title: put() maps 404 to NotFound — misleading for a write operation
description: map_client_error maps ClientError::APIError{code:404} to SecretError::NotFound. For get(), 404 means the secret key doesn't exist — correct. For put(), 404 means the KV mount doesn't exist (writing any path under a valid mount either creates or updates the secret; there is no '404 = path not found' for a write). A user who calls put() on a wrong mount gets 'secret not found', which implies the key they are writing is missing — a category error. The maintenance consequence: users will open issues asking why their put() fails with NotFound when the key they want to create doesn't exist yet.
